title: create_pack_with_valid_payload
description: Test creating a new pack with a valid payload including name, slug, type, and creatorId. Expect a 201 Created response with the created pack JSON including the slug.

title: create_pack_with_missing_required_field
description: Test creating a new pack with a payload missing a required field such as creatorId. Expect a 400 Bad Request response with a validation error.

title: create_pack_with_duplicate_slug
description: Test creating a new pack with a slug that already exists. Expect a 400 Bad Request response indicating a conflict error.

title: update_existing_pack
description: Test updating an existing pack by slug with valid updated fields. Expect a 200 OK response with updated pack JSON and subsequent GET to confirm updates.

title: update_non_existent_pack
description: Test updating a pack with a non-existent slug. Expect a 404 Not Found response.

title: suspend_existing_pack
description: Test suspending an existing pack by slug. Expect a 200 OK response with pack status set to 'suspended' and subsequent GET to confirm status.

title: unsuspend_existing_pack
description: Test unsuspending a suspended pack by slug. Expect a 200 OK response with pack status set to 'active' and subsequent GET to confirm status.

title: delete_existing_pack
description: Test deleting an existing pack by slug. Expect a 204 No Content response and subsequent GET to confirm the pack is not found.

title: create_asset_in_existing_pack
description: Test creating a new asset in an existing pack with valid payload including index, type, and data. Expect a 201 Created response with asset metadata.

title: resolve_asset_with_valid_selections
description: Test resolving an asset using the rules resolution API with a valid selections payload referencing packSlug and selectors. Expect a 200 OK response with the resolved asset payload.

title: update_srd_pack_metadata
description: Test updating metadata (name, description) of an SRD pack with non-semver version format (e.g. 5.1, 2024.1). Verify version validation accepts major.minor format, changes persist via GET, and original values are restored.
